rationale_for_baseline_tier: Benign tinea corporis — routine antifungal treatment and self-care with a secondary-infection safety-net; not urgent.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A lesion becoming hot/hard/swollen with thick pus, or fever
escalation_edges.communication_template: If a ring becomes hot, swollen, oozes thick pus, or you get a fever — see a doctor promptly.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Advise vet check of the kitten and household hygiene to prevent reinfection/spread.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is ringworm from your kitten. Stop the cortisone cream and apply the antifungal cream twice daily, extending 2 cm past the ring, for a week after it clears. Wash hands, don't share towels, and get the kitten vet-checked. Watch for hot/pus/fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP in 2 weeks; prompt review if secondary infection
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No urgent action needed unless secondary infection/fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document steroid-modified tinea corporis (tinea incognito) from a kitten, topical antifungal ± oral, and hygiene/source advice.

critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Tinea incognito spreads extensively, or a secondary infection is missed.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No antifungal or source/hygiene advice, allowing spread/reinfection.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot KOH-scrape remotely, but the annular scaly advancing border is characteristic.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as tinea corporis (stop steroid, antifungal past the border), advise source/hygiene, and safety-net for infection; oral antifungal if not clearing.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Continuing steroids or missing secondary infection.
